steps.title: Identify what the y-intercept represents
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is where the graph crosses the $y$-axis, which means $x=0$. Here $x$ is the number of months after the tablet was purchased. So $x=0$ means the moment it was purchased, before any months have passed.

steps.title: Read the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: At $x=0$, the graph is at $y=225$. So the point is $(0,225)$, meaning the estimated value at purchase was $\$225$.

steps.title: Interpret in context
steps.content: Choice A says the tablet was worth $\$225$ when it was purchased, which is exactly the meaning of the $y$-intercept. Choice B misuses the $24$-month value, choice C describes a decrease rather than the initial value, and choice D invents a yearly percentage rate. Choice A is correct.
